methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $157M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
BRINC Drones, a Series C defense and security drone company founded by Blake Resnick, is evaluated against a universe of 10 same-sector peers at comparable or adjacent stages, including 8 private companies and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The peer set spans valuations from $500M to $9.2B, with notable references including Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), and Vannevar Labs ($1.5B Series B).

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses BRINC's $157M total raised as a proxy valuation base, applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement to next-stage financing, yielding $267M. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company Defense & Security comp set produces a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B.

Triangulation of these two methods implies a low bound of $227M, median of $821M, and high bound of $1.6B. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects material uncertainty regarding BRINC's positioning relative to the peer cohort—particularly the presence of several Series D outliers valued above $4B. Key uncertainties include relative product maturity, customer concentration, and whether BRINC's technical or market position justifies a valuation toward the upper end of the defense/security peer range.
